title: DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES SERVICES - 211info
text: JACKSON COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers support services to individuals experiencing intellectual and/or developmental disabilities. Services can include case management, individual and family support plans, access to brokerage services for transportation, in-home support or residential placement, and employment and vocational services. 140 S Holly Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Individuals who have intellectual or development disablities and are U.S. citizens Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (541) 774-8205 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 774-8205 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or email for application FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Proof of citizenship, proof of address, and records of any/all psychological and medical assessments related to the disability. AREA SERVED: Jackson County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Between Holly and Ivy Streets off W 8th Avenue Services: Benefits and Services Assistance * Disabilities Issues Case/Care Management * Disabilities Issues Disability Related Center Based Employment Disability Related Parenting Programs Disability Related Transportation Home/Community Based Developmental Disabilities Programs Residential Placement Services for People with Disabilities Other Services Offered At This Location: MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - JACKSON COUNTY CRISIS LINE MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - PARENT CHILD INTERACTIVE THERAPY MENTAL HEALTH SUPPORT SERVICES - EARLY ASSESSMENT AND SUPPORT ALLIANCE SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - SYRINGE EXCHANGE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. JACKSON COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH
